title: The Problem Solvers: Michele Catasta at Replit
summary: Michele Catasta of Replit discusses his vision of democratizing software development through agentic building. Replit partnered with Anthropic in early 2024 to leverage Claude for coding capabilities, enabling over 40 million users to build software in natural language without coding experience.
writeup: A short interview with Replit's founder on how the platform uses Claude to eliminate coding's barrier to entry. The core argument: model intelligence is the engine, and Replit builds the car around it, so the choice of model is the product-defining decision. The conversation traces the arc from Sonnet 3.5 adoption in early 2024 through Sonnet 3.7 early access, which drove the same-day launch of Replit Agent V2 and a measurable marketing surge.
key_points: Replit chose Claude because the agent requires what the founder calls 'high IQ level': the raw reasoning power of the model is what collapses the skill gap for non-coders, making model selection a first-order product decision rather than an infrastructure one.

Anthropic granted early access to Sonnet 3.7 in early 2025; Replit rushed to build Agent V2 on it and coordinated a same-day launch with Anthropic's public release, producing a notable spike in user attention.

Replit has crossed 40 million registered users, a figure the founder cites to show the scale of people who now complete the full zero-to-one software build inside the platform, many of whom had never previously felt 'permission' to create software.

The founder frames the competitive moat not as the interface but as model quality: 'Anthropic kept the lead in having the best coding models on the market' since early 2024 is the stated reason the Sonnet line remains Replit's engine.

chapters.title: Sonnet 3.7 Early Access and Agent V2 Launch
